vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely obsolete due to severe specimen degradation. P3 (Pothos) is present but in a state of terminal decline. P1 (String of Nickels) is barely visible and likely non-viable. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are effectively lost, replaced by debris or necrotic remnants.

plant_audit.P1: String of Nickels: No longer identifiable as a living plant; appears as a dark, shriveled mass.
plant_audit.P2: Mexican Mint: No viable plant tissue detected.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos: Single leaf remains, showing signs of chlorosis and turgor loss. The white rabbit anchor is the only stable element.
plant_audit.P4: Silver Guest: No viable plant tissue detected.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp, potentially over-saturated, leading to the observed decay.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: None detected; the environment is currently hostile to secondary growth.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Presence of white, calcified debris (likely eggshell fragments) in the soil of the lower pot; significant loss of plant biomass across all containers.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-4: Minimal change; initial state of decline established.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-2: Progressive loss of leaf turgor in P3; P1 appears to be drying out.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_current: Total collapse of structural integrity; the environment has transitioned from a botanical collection to a state of biological failure.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of terminal collapse. The lack of light (despite the LED) and likely over-watering or substrate compaction has resulted in the death of the majority of the specimens. P3 is the only remaining biological anchor, and it is unlikely to recover without immediate intervention.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of white, crushed material (eggshells) in the soil is a non-standard intervention that may be altering soil pH or drainage, contributing to the observed failure.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic failure of the indoor botanical setup. Over the five-day observation period, the plants have transitioned from a state of distress to near-total mortality. The Pothos (P3) is the final remaining specimen, exhibiting severe signs of stress. The soil appears saturated, and the lack of healthy foliage suggests a systemic failure in care or environmental suitability.
